items.title: NT Labor MLA pledges change after another speeding fine revelation

children.content: An NT Labor politician has conceded he has an issue with speeding and will take driver refresher training, as a further infringement comes to light.

gallery.altText: An Aboriginal man, with black hair, sitting in a office chair in parliament, with a navy blue suit on, sullen expression.

thumbnail.altText: An Aboriginal man, with black hair, sitting in a office chair in parliament, with a navy blue suit on, sullen expression.

items.title: Millions spent on hardline school policies, but NT students not turning up

children.content: Just over half of all Aboriginal students in the NT are showing up for school, despite millions being spent on truancy policies.

image.altText: A group of primary aged kids in school uniforms walking on a red dirt road.

items.title: Qantas axes Alice Springs-Melbourne route amid high cost, falling demand

children.content: Qantas has axed its direct Alice Springs-Melbourne route after "many decades", and will also restrict flights between Darwin and Singapore to the peak tourist season.

image.altText: A photo of a Qantas plane flying in the sky.

items.title: NT outlaw bikie gang president sentenced to 10 years in prison

children.content: Secretly taped phone calls have captured the former president of the Mongols bikie gang's Northern Territory chapter discussing interstate drug deals while helping supply methamphetamine worth almost $800,000 to customers across the Top End.

gallery.altText: A police officer holds up a black vest reading Mongols MC Australia.

thumbnail.altText: A police officer holds up a black vest reading Mongols MC Australia.

items.title: Senior firefighter claims boss labelled her 'an overly emotional woman'

children.content: A former senior Northern Territory firefighter has claimed the service's deputy chief labelled her "an overly emotional woman" in a complaint to the territory's Anti-Discrimination Commission.

image.altText: A man in a dark blue uniform smiles for the camera.

items.title: Mobile CT scanner visits region where 'at least one smoker in every house'

children.content: A road train fitted with a battery-powered CT scanner is travelling across the NT outback to screen remote community residents for lung cancer, in the jurisdiction with Australia's highest diagnosis and mortality rates for the disease.

image.altText: A white road train parked in a parking lot with starts coming out of its side, and shelter. Sign reads 'Heart of Australia'

items.title: Hundreds killed as Venezuela struck by 'doublet' earthquake

children.content: The deadly one-two punch that toppled buildings in Venezuela's capital and beyond was a doublet earthquake, a pair of similar-sized quakes close in location and time.

image.altText: A woman walks in the rubble of a building, holding her hand up to her head

items.title: Another suspected H5 bird flu case detected in WA

children.content: Another suspected positive case of the H5 bird flu has been detected in Western Australia, marking the fifth confirmed or suspected case in Australia.

image.altText: A woman with shoulder-length hair is speaking to media.

items.title: Stefanovic's move to outsider politics follows a well-worn lucrative path

children.content: Listening to the culture war showman schtick he has so quickly adopted, Karl Stefanovic is now less a newsman than an ideological performer.

articles.title: Stefanovic thanks audience for 21 years on Today after 'tough' few days

children.content: Today show host Karl Stefanovic will leave Channel Nine after a controversial podcast interview with British far-right figure Tommy Robinson.

items.title: 'ISIS bride' charged with slavery offences is granted bail

items.title: Position switch the latest chapter in Bulldogs' experiment against Titans

items.title: How China's new 'ethnic unity' law could target people in Australia

items.title: Why the Moana live action movie has come so soon

items.title: How I save money by storing and cooking things differently

items.title: Hobart stadium developer not supportive of waterfront precinct proposal

items.title: Judge rejects role of Ozempic in grandmother's roadside stabbing attack

children.content: A 60-year-old woman who stabbed her grandson and daughter-in-law has been sentenced to jail. Her defence team had argued she was suffering sudden and extreme anger partially induced by weight-loss medication.

image.altText: A group of police in uniform stand beside a four wheel drive car on the side of the road.

items.title: No-show cuttlefish result in million-dollar loss for regional SA town

children.content: Whyalla has been forced to wind back services and let staff let go as its tourism industry suffers million-dollar losses from cuttlefish not making their usual migration.

items.title: Man sentenced for 'execution' of police officer serving repossession notice

image.altText: Leigh Sushames looks at the camera.

items.title: SA Police officers 'did not act appropriately' in handling of DV survivor's case
